captions.hamiltonian: Total (H = K-hat + V-hat) = the full energy the shot evolves under.
captions.kinetic: Kinetic (K-hat) = how movement opens scoring lanes.
captions.potential: Potential (V-hat) = how the defense walls those lanes off.
captions.sigma: Sigma = the clock speed of the evolution (how fast the odds settle).

physics_explainer.defensive_read: Current defensive pressure reads as light.
physics_explainer.headline: What the possession math is actually doing
physics_explainer.simple: Think of the next shot like a coin that has not landed yet. Fast, well-spaced ball movement tilts the odds toward a made 2 or 3, while a defender crowding the shooter tilts them back toward a miss. The numbers below are just that tilt, written out.
physics_explainer.technical: H = K-hat + V-hat. K-hat encodes velocity-driven off-diagonal coupling (speed, burst, spacing x shot-zone bias) that moves amplitude into the 2- and 3-point states. V-hat is a diagonal defensive potential barrier from rim/closeout pressure that suppresses those transitions. The state evolves under i*sigma*dPsi/dt = H*Psi, and the reported probabilities are |Psi|^2 of the evolved state.
